item.title: Maps show wildfire smoke forecast, air quality alerts in swath of U.S.
item.description: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week.

item.title: Ukrainians protest over Zelenskyy's move to oust popular defense chief
item.description: Ukrainians are demonstrating in Kyiv and senior figures announcing their resignations over President Volodymyr Zelenksyy's move to oust his popular defense chief.

item.title: U.S. and Iran launch new attacks and hurl threats as war intensifies
item.description: Iran and the U.S. announce new attacks and match each other's threats as the war ramps up over control of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Extended interview: Sons of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, man killed by ICE in Houston, speak out
item.description: Ronaldo Salgado and Lorenzo Salgado Jr. remember their father, Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, who was shot and killed by an ICE agent in Houston on July 7 after living in the U.S. for 35 years. ICE has claimed he "weaponized" his van during a traffic stop, an allegation disputed by his family.

item.title: Half of Young Adults Still Live at Home | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
item.description: Half of American adults under 30 are now living with their parents. Is this the new normal, or just a temporary consequence of today's housing market? Regardless, Jill advises young adults living at home to at least offer to chip in financially. Plus, Mark explains why obsessing over minimizing taxes can lead to costly investing mistakes, and Jill shares her thoughts on how friends with different income levels should approach splitting the cost of dinners, vacations, and group outings.

item.title: DHS could weigh use of Medicaid, housing help in green card decisions
item.description: Immigration officers could weigh use of Medicaid, food aid and housing help in green card decisions after Trump administration rescinds Biden-era public charge rule.

item.title: Carbon monoxide found in SUV where 3 died, 2 sickened in Ohio, official says
item.description: Officials in Toledo, Ohio, say the presence of carbon monoxide was confirmed in a parked vehicle where five people were found unresponsive and three of them died.

item.title: Councilwoman shot, husband killed in Mexico near U.S. border
item.description: Maria de Jesus Quijada was shot at while traveling in a vehicle with her family. Her husband did not survive.

item.title: Trump to allege Chinese meddling in U.S. elections in primetime speech, sources say
item.description: Part of President Trump's speech Thursday night is expected to touch on previously unreported alleged Chinese meddling in U.S. elections, according to sources familiar with the matter.

item.title: From Balogun's red card to an upset run, some of the World Cup's biggest stories
item.description: Spain is looking to secure a World Cup victory for the first time in 16 years, and Argentina enters Sunday hoping to become back-to-back champions.

item.title: Moms of Black newborns often flagged to police over drug use claims
item.description: In 14,000 cases, Black families were flagged to law enforcement over unverified allegations that often begin with unreliable hospital drug tests.

item.title: U.S. to impose 25% tariffs on Brazilian imports over unfair trade practices
item.description: The United States is imposing 25% tariffs on imports from Brazil after finding a range of what it deemed unfair trade practices by the world's 10th-biggest economy.

item.title: Coast Guard suspends search for 3 missing boaters in San Francisco Bay
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard announced Wednesday that the search for three boaters missing after their vessel sank in San Francisco Bay will be suspended at sunset.

item.title: What credit score do you need for the lowest mortgage rates right now? Experts weigh in
item.description: Having a high credit score can translate into big savings on your mortgage loan, but how high does it need to be?

item.title: 1 dead, 3 missing after boat sinks near Alcatraz Island in San Francisco Bay
item.description: One person died and three were missing in San Francisco Bay on Tuesday after a boat with 20 people on board sank near Alcatraz Island, authorities said.

item.title: Volunteers deciphering first-person Revolutionary War accounts written in cursive
item.description: Americans are putting their cursive reading skills to work in service of their country. "Citizen archivists" are helping the National Archives transcribe some 2.5 million pages of handwritten Revolutionary War pension files. Natalie Brand reports.

item.title: Argentina fans rejoice as team makes it to World Cup final for second time in a row
item.description: Argentina will have a chance to defend its World Cup title after defeating England 2-1 in the semifinal. This is the second time in a row Argentina has reached the final. CBS News' Elizabeth Campbell reports on the excitement from fans.

item.title: U.S., Iran in a "new phase" of the conflict, retired Navy vice admiral explains
item.description: U.S. Central Command confirmed it began a second wave of strikes against Iran on Wednesday, following a round of attacks earlier in the day. Retired Navy Vice Adm. Robert Murrett joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Inside a recycling firm for electronic waste
item.description: As the technological revolution kicks into overdrive, the world is generating more electronic waste. One company is trying to reboot how we recycle our old tech. Evyn Moon takes us inside the process.

item.title: National blood emergency declared as American Red Cross calls for donations
item.description: The American Red Cross is announcing a national blood emergency with officials warning that hospitals are in dire need of blood products for emergencies and surgeries. Gwen Baumgardner has more.

item.title: Sons say they learned father was killed by ICE through video, news reports
item.description: Ronaldo Salgado said he learned his father, 52-year-old Mexican immigrant Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, had been shot by an ICE officer in Houston last week through a video posted online that depicted him "screaming" for help.

item.title: How drones are reshaping modern warfare in Ukraine, Middle East
item.description: Drones have become a defining feature of the conflict between Russia and Ukraine. They've also been used extensively in the Middle East amid the war in Iran. Kelsey Baker, military affairs reporter at Business Insider, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: 2 families sue hospital after Colorado man claims he was switched at birth
item.description: Two years ago, Jeremy Morrison took a DNA test and found out his parents were not biological actual parents.

item.title: Who could replace Lindsey Graham in the Senate after his sudden death?
item.description: South Carolina Sen. Lindsey Graham's sudden death late Saturday has set off a scramble for who will succeed him in the Senate.

item.title: U.S. citizen departs Iran after being barred from leaving for over a year
item.description: A U.S.-Iranian woman who was trapped in Iran on allegations of espionage and collaborating with a hostile state that her attorney called "bogus" has departed the country, CBS News learned Wednesday.

item.title: Biddeford, Maine, mayor on ICE shooting, community's response
item.description: Liam LaFountain, mayor of Biddeford, Maine, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss his town's response to Monday's incident where an ICE officer shot and killed a man.

item.title: U.S., Iran exchange strikes for fifth straight day, Trump says, "they better behave"
item.description: The U.S. launched back-to-back strikes on Iran Wednesday, marking a fifth straight day of attacks. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: DHS says all ICE arrest teams will have agent with body camera, Trump reinstates ICE traffic stops
item.description: After federal immigration agents shot and killed two people this month, the Department of Homeland Security now says every arrest team going forward will have at least one officer wearing a body camera. Also on Wednesday, President Trump overturned an order that would've stopped ICE agents from performing some traffic stops. CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga has more details on ICE policy changes.

item.title: 7/15: CBS Evening News
item.description: Canada wildfires darken U.S. skies, trigger air quality alerts; senators on both sides of the aisle question Todd Blanche's independence.

item.title: Gordie Howe Bridge connecting Detroit, Canada expected to open in late July
item.description: The 1.5-mile-long bridge, which spans the Detroit River and connects the Motor City with Windsor, Ontario, is expected to open on July 27.
